sections.paragraph:
Σαμφουριν indecl. Samphurin ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τ. χώραν Σαμφουριν ἐγγὺς τῆς ἐρήμου εἰς Ἐφραὶμ λεγομένην πόλιν J 11:54 D (cp. Josh 19:19 A ʼ Αφεραϊμ). S. Dalman, Orte u. Wege Jesus3 1924, 233 [Eng. tr. 219]; RBrown, comm. ad loc. perhaps a corruption of שֵׁם אֶפְרַיִם ‘whose name is Ephraim’. On Sepphoris, which some consider the referent in the v.l., s. PECS 827f, w. lit.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Eng. tr. Eng. tr. = English translation

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

PECS PECS = Princeton Encyclopedia of Classical Sites—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 912
